description: All cabinetry installed, adjusted, and client-approved before countertop templating and appliance set-out begin.

categories.name: Pre-Install Verification
items: Cabinet shop drawings reviewed and approved by client (door style, finish, interior layout, hardware placement)
Cabinet delivery confirmed received in full — all boxes and components checked against packing list before installer arrival
All boxes inspected for shipping damage; damaged or incorrect pieces documented and replacements ordered before install begins
Walls and floors in cabinet zones confirmed plumb, level, and flat within 3/16" over 8 ft; shims and blocking plan confirmed with installer
Blocking installed in all upper cabinet walls per cabinet layout drawing (typically 2× blocking between studs at specified heights)
Rough plumbing, electrical, and HVAC rough-in confirmed complete and inspected before cabinets close off access
Cabinet hardware (knobs, pulls, hinges) confirmed on-site and matched to approved selection sheet

categories.name: Installation
items: Upper cabinets installed first; all secured to wall studs or blocking with minimum #8 × 2-1/2" screws, not drywall anchors
All upper cabinet runs level across full run within 1/8"; shims concealed or scribed, no exposed gaps at wall
Base cabinets installed level across full run within 1/8"; toe kicks aligned and continuous, no rocking on floor
Cabinet face frames tight at all joints; stile-to-stile seams at joined cabinets flush within 1/32" and clamped/screwed per manufacturer spec
All fillers, scribe moldings, and end panels cut, fit, and secured — no raw MDF edges exposed at wall or ceiling interfaces
Sink base confirmed positioned over drain and supply rough-in; no cabinet modification that violates structural integrity of box
Island or peninsula cabinets secured to subfloor per cabinet manufacturer's anchoring method; no movement or racking
Range, refrigerator, dishwasher, and appliance openings measured and confirmed against confirmed appliance specs before install is finalized

categories.name: Doors, Drawers & Hardware
items: All cabinet doors hang plumb and level; gap between door and frame consistent at 1/16" ± 1/32" on all sides
All doors and drawer fronts aligned across adjacent cabinets — reveals consistent, no stair-stepping across a run
All hinges adjusted to three-axis spec (vertical, horizontal, depth); soft-close function confirmed on every door
All drawer boxes fully operational — slides extend to full extension, soft-close engages, no binding or mistrack
All pulls and knobs installed at uniform placement per approved hardware template; pilot holes clean, no tear-out
All interior shelf pins installed, shelves seated level and confirmed to rated load (pull test by hand, no deflection on span)
All doors and drawers opened and closed minimum three times by superintendent and confirmed smooth with no rub, catch, or squeak

categories.name: Quality & Punch
items: All finish surfaces (doors, drawer fronts, face frames, end panels) inspected under raking light for scratches, dents, finish holidays, or color inconsistency
All cut edges at scribe, filler, and toe kick confirmed clean — no chipping on painted or thermofoil finish, no raw substrate visible
All cabinet interiors clean and free of sawdust, hardware packaging, and debris
Caulk line at cabinet-to-wall and cabinet-to-ceiling interfaces confirmed uniform, tooled, and painted-grade (paintable caulk, not silicone) where painted finish will be applied
Under-cabinet lighting rough-in (if applicable) confirmed stubbed to correct location and accessible post-install
Any items requiring cabinet shop warranty service (damaged doors, wrong sizes, defective hinges) documented on punch list with ETA for resolution

categories.name: Sign-Off & Next-Phase Handoff
items: Superintendent final walk of all cabinet zones completed and sign-off recorded in project management system
Client walkthrough completed; client signs Cabinet Phase Acceptance form acknowledging layout, finish, and function meet selections and shop drawings
Any open punch items documented in writing with responsible party and due date before countertop template is scheduled
Countertop fabricator notified that cabinets are level, complete, and ready for template — template date locked on schedule
Cabinet installer's final invoice approved and submitted for draw processing only after client sign-off is received
